metadata_information.title: Preschool education in Brazil : does public supply crowd out private enrollment
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Preschool education in Brazil : does public supply crowd out private enrollment

document_description.abstract: This paper examines whether an expansion in the supply of public preschool crowds out private enrollment. The paper uses rich data for municipalities in Brazil from 2000 to 2006, where federal transfers to local governments change discontinuously with given population thresholds. The results from a regression-discontinuity design reveal that larger federal transfers lead to a significant expansion of local public preschool services, but show no evidence of crowding out of private enrollment. This finding is consistent with a theory in which households differ in their willingness to pay for preschool services, and private suppliers optimally adjust prices in response to an expansion of lower-quality, free-of-charge public supply.
